summary: Separate workspace selection from terminal activation so terminals stay alive across dashboard tabs and only close when a workspace is explicitly deactivated or the app closes.

review_items.summary: Clicking a workspace selects and activates it, while the active terminal runtime is tracked independently from the selected workspace view state.
Switching to Files, Vault, Audio, MCPs, or Settings hides the terminal surface instead of unmounting it, preventing tab navigation from closing terminal processes.
Workspace settings expose activate/deactivate controls plus a persisted auto-activate workspace choice, including no default workspace.
Terminal cleanup remains tied to terminal close, explicit workspace deactivation, and app shutdown; auth, billing, hosted API, and database contracts are unchanged.

rules: Workspace terminals must remain mounted and running when navigating between dashboard tabs.
A workspace is activated when its terminal runtime is active; closed workspaces keep their directory label but do not light the activation bar.
Deactivating a workspace must close and clean up its terminal panes by unmounting that workspace's terminal runtime.
A user may persist an auto-activate workspace or choose no default workspace; no default should open the app without automatically starting terminals.
Creating the first workspace should make it the default auto-activate workspace.
The terminal runtime lifecycle must not alter auth checks, billing gates, desktop session behavior, hosted API contracts, or database behavior.

patches.summary: Track activated workspace state, persist auto-activate workspace preference, render a persistent terminal layer, and add activate/deactivate controls.
Add selected/activating/activated workspace rail styling and workspace view stack styles that hide inactive tabs without unmounting the terminal runtime.

compatibility.notes: Local desktop UI/runtime behavior only; no API, auth, billing, or SQL contract changes.
